subject: Geology
title: Remote Sensing
description: A short lesson to help you apply Remote Sensing.

hook.text: Satellites can detect mineral deposits, fault lines, and land movement from hundreds of kilometers above.

buildup.text: Remote sensing uses satellites, radar, and aerial photography to study Earth's surface without direct contact.

discovery.text: Different minerals reflect light differently. Multispectral imaging can map geology from orbit.

twist.text: InSAR radar detects ground movement of just millimeters. It can spot a volcano inflating before it erupts.

climax.text: Remote sensing lets geologists study the whole planet at once. It turned geology into a global science.

punchline.text: Now we read the Earth from space.
